perturbation.original_text: During the Term and subject to the limitations set forth in Paragraphs 9 and 10, HDS shall have the right to use the name, image, likeness, characterization, visual and audio representation of Celebrity ("Celebrity Attributes") in connection with HDS' product, Clotamin, in the Territory as follows: A. In a television commercial (specific spot length to be mutually agreed upon) promoting Clotamin ("Commercial") aired specifically in the following three (3) television markets: (1) Washington, DC Metro Area; (2) Florida; and (3) Texas (collectively "Markets"); B. On HDS' website (www.clotamin.corn) ("Website"); and C. In Clotamin-related press releases.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Term and subject to the limitations set forth in Paragraphs 9 and 10, HDS shall have the right to use the name, image, likeness, characterization, visual and audio representation of Celebrity (“Celebrity Persona”) in connection with HDS' product, Clotamin, in the Territory as follows: A. In a television commercial (specific spot length to be mutually agreed upon) promoting Clotamin (“Commercial”) aired specifically in the following three (3) television markets: (1) Washington, DC Metro Area; (2) Florida; and (3) Texas (collectively “Markets”); B. On HDS' website (www.clotamin.corn) (“Website”); and C. In Clotamin-related press releases.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Celebrity Attributes' is changed to 'Celebrity Persona'. This change introduces a perturbation because the contract later refers specifically to 'Celebrity Attributes' in Paragraph 9 and 10. By redefining it here, the contract introduces uncertainty as to whether those later sections still apply. The contradiction appears because the contract uses both terms, 'Celebrity Attributes' and 'Celebrity Persona', without explicitly stating they are the same, leading to potential disputes over the scope of rights granted to HDS. This is directly contradicting in section 9 and 10, where it's reffering 'Celebrity Attributes'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All HDS' uses of Celebrity Attributes in connection with the Commercial and/or press releases shall be subject to the prior written approval of Celebrity via his agent, CSE. Said written approval must be given within five (5) business days of CSE's receipt or said usage shall be deemed unapproved. Any such usage featuring Celebrity in the Commercial and/or press releases shall be and remain the property of HDS; however, HDS shall have the right to use said Commercial and/or press releases solely as outlined in Paragraph 3 and only during the Term. Celebrity may use said materials in whole or in part solely for the purpose of presenting Celebrity's work in Celebrity's personal portfolio, website or otherwise and/or on Celebrity's agent's website. Such usage may not be sold or transferred.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Celebrity Attributes' to 'Celebrity Persona' introduces a contradiction. Paragraph 9 and 10 (represented by the contradicted text) specifically refer to 'Celebrity Attributes'. By changing the term used in Paragraph 3, the contract creates ambiguity regarding whether the restrictions and requirements in Paragraph 9 and 10 still apply to the 'Celebrity Persona'. This is a strong contradiction because it affects the rights and obligations related to the use of the celebrity's likeness, potentially leading to disputes over approvals and ownership, because it's no longer clear if 'Celebrity Attributes' and 'Celebrity Persona' are the same.

perturbation.original_text: Upon request by HDS, one (1) production session to be used for the production of the Commercial ("Production Session"). The location, date and time of the Production Session shall be mutually agreed upon by Celebrity and HDS. In the event the Production Session exceeds eight (8) hours in duration HDS and Celebrity will negotiate in good faith additional compensation to Celebrity for time in excess of eight (8) hours.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon request by HDS, one (1) recording session to be used for the production of the Commercial (“Recording Session”). The location, date and time of the Recording Session shall be mutually agreed upon by Celebrity and HDS. In the event the Recording Session exceeds eight (8) hours in duration HDS and Celebrity will negotiate in good faith additional compensation to Celebrity for time in excess of eight (8) hours.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Production Session' is changed to 'Recording Session'. Later in section 5B (1), there's a refference to Production Session, which can be confused due to change to Recording Session, on which section it's really refferencing to. Due to this change, this introduces a perturbation in how payments are being made for the sessions and the section can be contradictive if it's refferencing Recording Session or Production Session.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Cash Payment: (1) HDS will provide payment of Fifty Thousand and NO/100 Dollars ($50,000) made payable to Celebrity's agent. CSE, as follows $10,000 Within ten (10) days of the parties execution of this Agreement $15,000 Within 60 days of the parties execution of this agreement $25,000* Ten (10) days prior to the Production Session [*Should Company decide not to conduct the Production Session, then the $25,000 allocated for said Production Session ("Production Fee") will not be owed to Celebrity and notice should be given to CSE as soon as reasonably known by Company if Company does not intend to conduct the Production Session. However, if Company schedules the Production Session, then said Production Fee is non-refundable regardless if the Production Session occurs. ] (a) CSE, shall invoice HDS for these fees and HDS shall pay such invoice within ten (10) business days following receipt of the CSE invoice. (b) Payments shall be made to CSE and delivered to 600 Galleria Parkway, Suite 1900, Atlanta, Georgia 30339.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Production Session' to 'Recording Session' creates a misaligned terminology issue. The contradicted text explicitly refers to 'Production Session' and allocates a 'Production Fee' associated with it. By changing the term to 'Recording Session' in the modified text, it creates ambiguity about whether the 'Production Fee' in the contradicted text applies to the newly named 'Recording Session'. This ambiguity constitutes a strong in-text contradiction because it directly impacts the payment terms and creates uncertainty regarding which session the fee is associated with, potentially leading to a dispute. It meets the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' as the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them and therefore leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Celebrity relies upon HDS' skill and judgment and also upon the following representations of HDS which shall be in effect throughout the term of this Agreement: A. HDS' products will be merchantable and fit for the purpose for which they are intended, and B. HDS' products will conform at all times to all applicable federal, state and local laws, rules, regulations, ordinances, and other enactments and industry standards, including, but not limited to, those relating to product safety.
perturbation.changed_text: Celebrity relies upon HDS' skill and judgment and also upon the following representations of HDS which shall be in effect throughout the term of this Agreement: A. HDS' goods will be merchantable and fit for the purpose for which they are intended, and B. HDS' products will conform at all times to all applicable federal, state and local laws, rules, regulations, ordinances, and other enactments and industry standards, including, but not limited to, those relating to product safety.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'products' in Section 12A is changed to 'goods'. Section 13 refers to HDS' product liability. By changing 'products' to 'goods' in 12A, it is unclear whether the indemnity clause covers liability arising from both 'goods' and 'products' or just the latter, creating uncertainty regarding the scope of indemnification. This is directly contradicting section 13, where it's reffering 'HDS product liability'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: HDS shall be solely responsible for all liability arising out of production, distribution and sale of its product. HDS hereby agrees to indemnify, defend and hold harmless Celebrity, his agents, representatives and employees (referred to collectively as "Celebrity Indemnities") from and against any and all claims, actions, causes or action, damages, injuries, expenses, liabilities (joint and several), penalties fines, attorneys fees, court costs, and any other expenses incurred by Celebrity Indemnities arising out of (1) breach by HDS of any of the terms, representations or warranties made by HDS in this Agreement; or (2) HDS product liability or trademark patent or other proprietary right infringement; or (3) errors, omissions, fraudulent or negligent acts by HDS, its employees, agents or subcontractors in connection with (i) any advertising featuring Celebrity; (ii) with the performance of HDS' duties and obligations under this Agreement; (iii) with the production, distribution, promotion, marketing and sales of products including related product packaging; and/or (iv) with the operation and management of its production and distribution facilities, however caused, HDS shall not be obligated to indemnify Celebrity with respect to damages which are the result of the active negligence or willful misconduct of Celebrity.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses the term 'products' consistently throughout section 12A. The changed text replaces 'products' with 'goods'. Section 13, the 'Contradicted Text', refers specifically to 'HDS product liability' and 'production, distribution, promotion, marketing and sales of products'. By changing 'products' to 'goods' in section 12A, the scope of HDS's liability and indemnity obligations becomes ambiguous. It is now unclear whether section 13 applies to 'goods' as well as 'products', creating an in-text contradiction and uncertainty regarding indemnification, since HDS's liability arising from 'goods' is not mentioned in section 13, leading to a direct conflict. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the contract uses 'goods' and 'products' in a way that creates conflicting obligations.
